steps.title: Read the figure
steps.content: Transversal $t$ crosses line $r$, forming angles of $107^{\circ}$ (left of $t$) and $73^{\circ}$ (right of $t$). At line $s$, the angle $x^{\circ}$ is on the left side of $t$, in the same relative position as the $107^{\circ}$ angle at line $r$.

steps.title: Test all four answer choices using corresponding angles
steps.content:
• Choice A — $90$: Corresponding angles with parallel lines are equal, not necessarily 90°. The angle at $r$ is 107°, so $x = 90$ would require supplementary angles to be 90° each, implying $t \perp r$ — but $107^{\circ} \neq 90^{\circ}$. ✗
  • Choice B — $180$: $x = 180$ is impossible for a single interior angle. ✗
  • Choice C — $17$: $x = 17$ does not match any standard angle relationship: it is neither the corresponding angle (107°), alternate interior angle (73°), nor a supplement or difference that follows from parallel line theorems. ✗
  • Choice D — $107$: $x^{\circ}$ is in the same position relative to line $s$ as the 107° angle is to line $r$. Corresponding angles formed by a transversal crossing parallel lines are equal, so $x = 107$. ✓

steps.title: Conclusion
steps.content: Since $r \parallel s$, corresponding angles are equal: $x^{\circ} = 107^{\circ}$. That matches Choice D — $107$.
